title: [userkeys]
text: [userkeys] You can add user-defined keyboard shortcut keys for various Uniface IDE actions or your applications in the [userkeys] section of the usys.ini file. Description [userkeys] action=key_combination Copy You can change the key_combination as per your requirement, but the action arguments are fixed. Example : code.uppercase=Ctrl+U Settings The following section displays the default supported [userkeys] section settings. Explain this code [userkeys] nav.ubar=Alt+D nav.message.frame=Ctrl+M editor.refresh=F5 editor.close=Ctrl+F4 editor.close.all=Ctrl+Shift+F4 editor.next=Ctrl+Tab editor.previous=Ctrl+Shift+Tab build.compile=Ctrl+F7 build.test=Ctrl+F5 build.compile.test=Ctrl+Shift+F7 code.comment=Ctrl+K code.uncomment=Ctrl+Shift+K code.uppercase=Ctrl+U code.lowercase=Ctrl+Shift+U code.formatselection=Alt+F code.formatdocument=Alt+Shift+F code.search=Ctrl+F code.searchandreplace=Ctrl+H code.gotoline=Ctrl+G Copy Related Links Comment and uncomment code You can comment and uncomment codes in the Uniface IDE using the IDE code editor popup menu. Uppercase and lowercase code You can uppercase and lowercase code in the Uniface IDE using the IDE code editor popup menu. Adding user-defined keyboard shortcuts in the IDE You can use the following default shortcut keys in the IDE to navigate inside the editor or trigger various supported actions with a keyboard, such as compile a current object or compile and test an object.
